hints: This Italian Renaissance polymath was born in 1452 near Florence.
He filled notebooks with sketches of inventions, anatomy, and engineering ideas.
He painted both The Last Supper and a portrait with a famously enigmatic smile.
That portrait is the Mona Lisa, now in the Louvre.
First name: Leonardo. Last name starts with 'V'.
baseClueFact: A Renaissance master born in Tuscany, Italy in 1452.

hints: This ancient ruler is one of history's most famous monarchs.
She was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt.
She is strongly associated with Julius Caesar and Mark Antony.
She died in 30 BC after Antony's defeat by Octavian.
Name: Cleopatra.
baseClueFact: An ancient Egyptian queen who ruled in the 1st century BC.

hints: This military leader rose during the French Revolution.
He became Emperor of the French in 1804 and redrew much of Europe's map.
He is linked to the Napoleonic Code and a disastrous Russian campaign.
He was defeated at Waterloo in 1815 and exiled to Saint Helena.
First name: Napoleon. Last name starts with 'B'.
baseClueFact: A French military genius who became emperor in the early 19th century.

hints: This scientist was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
Born in Warsaw, she later built her career in Paris.
She discovered polonium and radium and helped define radioactivity research.
She remains the only person awarded Nobels in two different scientific fields.
First name: Marie. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: A Polish-French physicist and chemist who pioneered radioactivity research.

hints: This 20th-century physicist is one of the best known scientists ever.
Born in Germany in 1879, he later moved to the United States.
He developed the special and general theories of relativity.
His equation E = mc² became the symbol of modern physics.
First name: Albert. Last name starts with 'E'.
baseClueFact: A German-born theoretical physicist who transformed modern physics.

hints: This English writer is often called the greatest playwright in the language.
He wrote tragedies, comedies, histories, and more than 150 sonnets.
He was associated with the Globe Theatre in London.
His works include Hamlet, Macbeth, Othello, and Romeo and Juliet.
First name: William. Last name starts with 'S'.
baseClueFact: An English playwright and poet from Stratford-upon-Avon, born in 1564.

hints: This English mathematician and physicist was central to the Scientific Revolution.
He wrote Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica.
He formulated the laws of motion and universal gravitation.
A falling apple is famously linked with his thinking about gravity.
First name: Isaac. Last name starts with 'N'.
baseClueFact: An English mathematician and physicist who described gravity and motion.

hints: This Italian scientist was born in Pisa in 1564.
He improved telescopes and used them for major astronomical observations.
He supported heliocentrism and clashed with church authorities.
He spent his final years under house arrest after an Inquisition trial.
First name: Galileo. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: An Italian astronomer who championed the heliocentric model.

hints: This Roman general and statesman helped end the Roman Republic.
He conquered Gaul and later crossed the Rubicon.
His civil war victories made him dictator.
He was assassinated on the Ides of March in 44 BC.
First name: Julius. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: A Roman general and statesman critical to the fall of the Republic.

hints: This conqueror built one of history's largest empires before age 33.
He was king of Macedon and a student of Aristotle.
He defeated the Persian Empire in a series of major campaigns.
His empire stretched from Greece and Egypt to northwestern India.
First name: Alexander. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: An ancient Macedonian king who built a vast empire.

hints: This Italian Renaissance artist excelled in sculpture, painting, and architecture.
He created the marble David and the Pietà.
He painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel.
He also contributed to the design of St Peter's Basilica.
Name: Michelangelo.
baseClueFact: An Italian Renaissance master active in Florence and Rome.

hints: This inventor and engineer is closely tied to modern electrical power systems.
Born in the Balkans, he later emigrated to the United States.
He became the great champion of alternating current power.
His rivalry with Thomas Edison is often called the War of the Currents.
First name: Nikola. Last name starts with 'T'.
baseClueFact: A Serbian-American inventor who helped develop AC electricity.

hints: This English naturalist changed biology forever.
He sailed on HMS Beagle and studied species across the world.
His observations helped him formulate natural selection.
He published On the Origin of Species in 1859.
First name: Charles. Last name starts with 'D'.
baseClueFact: An English naturalist who proposed evolution by natural selection.

hints: This political leader is revered as a father of modern India.
He trained as a lawyer and developed strategies of nonviolent resistance.
He led campaigns such as the Salt March against British rule.
India gained independence in 1947, the year before his assassination.
First name: Mahatma. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: An Indian independence leader who championed nonviolence.

hints: This American civil rights leader became one of the defining figures of the 20th century.
He was a Baptist minister from Atlanta, Georgia.
He helped lead the Montgomery Bus Boycott and the March on Washington.
His best-known speech begins with the words 'I Have a Dream.'
First name: Martin. Last name starts with 'J'.
baseClueFact: An American civil rights activist and minister.

hints: This medieval French heroine said she received divine visions.
She emerged during the Hundred Years' War.
She inspired French victories, especially around Orléans.
Captured by enemies, she was tried and burned in 1431.
First name: Joan. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: A French peasant girl turned military heroine.

hints: This American president led his country through its bloodiest internal war.
He was born in Kentucky and largely self-educated.
He issued the Emancipation Proclamation during the Civil War.
He was assassinated in 1865, days after Union victory.
First name: Abraham. Last name starts with 'L'.
baseClueFact: The 16th U.S. president who led the Union through the Civil War.

hints: This Austrian composer was a child prodigy.
He performed across Europe before reaching adulthood.
He wrote symphonies, operas, chamber works, and concertos at astonishing speed.
His operas include The Magic Flute and Don Giovanni.
Name: Mozart.
baseClueFact: An Austrian musical prodigy of the Classical era.

hints: This Victorian author wrote some of the most famous English novels ever.
Many of his works first appeared in serial form.
He created characters like Oliver Twist, Ebenezer Scrooge, and Miss Havisham.
His books include Great Expectations and A Tale of Two Cities.
First name: Charles. Last name starts with 'D'.
baseClueFact: A Victorian English novelist known for social critique and memorable characters.

hints: This mathematician is often called the first computer programmer.
She collaborated with Charles Babbage on the Analytical Engine.
She described how a machine could follow a sequence of operations.
Her notes included an algorithm for calculating Bernoulli numbers.
First name: Ada. Last name starts with 'L'.
baseClueFact: A 19th-century mathematician associated with the first computer algorithm.

hints: This Athenian philosopher left no writings of his own.
He is known largely through the works of Plato and Xenophon.
He questioned assumptions through relentless dialogue.
He was sentenced to death and drank hemlock.
Name: Socrates.
baseClueFact: A classical Greek philosopher famous for dialectic questioning.

hints: This philosopher founded the Academy in Athens.
He wrote dialogues featuring his teacher Socrates.
His works include the Republic and the Symposium.
He had an enormous influence on Western philosophy and political thought.
Name: Plato.
baseClueFact: A Greek philosopher and student of Socrates.

hints: This philosopher studied under Plato and later tutored Alexander the Great.
He wrote on logic, ethics, politics, biology, and rhetoric.
His school in Athens was called the Lyceum.
He shaped philosophy and science for centuries.
Name: Aristotle.
baseClueFact: A Greek philosopher whose work influenced many fields.

hints: This Chinese thinker emphasized ethics, family duty, and proper conduct.
His ideas were preserved by later followers in sayings and dialogues.
He stressed social harmony and respect within relationships.
His teachings became central to East Asian political and moral traditions.
Name: Confucius.
baseClueFact: A Chinese philosopher whose teachings shaped East Asia.

hints: This ancient Chinese sage is traditionally linked to Daoism.
He is associated with a compact text of paradoxical wisdom.
That work is the Tao Te Ching.
He teaches harmony with the Way rather than forceful control.
Name: Laozi.
baseClueFact: A legendary Chinese philosopher associated with Daoism.

hints: This strategist wrote one of the world's most famous military treatises.
His text emphasizes deception, planning, and winning efficiently.
The book is called The Art of War.
It has influenced generals, politicians, and business thinkers for centuries.
First name: Sun. Last name starts with 'T'.
baseClueFact: An ancient Chinese military theorist and writer.

hints: This ruler unified a major East Asian civilization under one empire.
He became the first emperor of a unified China in 221 BC.
He standardized weights, measures, and writing across his realm.
He is linked with the early Great Wall and the Terracotta Army.
First name: Qin. Last name starts with 'H'.
baseClueFact: The first emperor of a unified China.

hints: This South Asian emperor once expanded an enormous empire through warfare.
After the brutal Kalinga War, he embraced Buddhism.
He promoted moral edicts carved on pillars and rocks.
His lion capital later became a national symbol of India.
Name: Ashoka.
baseClueFact: A Mauryan emperor known for embracing Buddhism after conquest.

hints: This Athenian statesman helped shape his city during its golden age.
He promoted democracy, public building, and naval power.
The Parthenon was built during his leadership.
He gave a famous funeral oration during the Peloponnesian War.
Name: Pericles.
baseClueFact: An Athenian leader of the 5th century BC.

hints: This Carthaginian commander became Rome's great nightmare.
He led troops, cavalry, and elephants across the Alps.
He won major victories such as Cannae during the Second Punic War.
Despite his brilliance, Carthage ultimately lost the war.
Name: Hannibal.
baseClueFact: A Carthaginian general famed for crossing the Alps.

hints: This ruler transformed Rome after years of civil war.
He defeated Antony and Cleopatra and became Rome's first emperor.
He launched a long period later called the Pax Romana.
He was the adopted heir of Julius Caesar.
Name: Augustus.
baseClueFact: The first Roman emperor.

hints: This Roman emperor is remembered both for rule and reflection.
He belonged to the group later called the Five Good Emperors.
He wrote private philosophical notes during military campaigns.
Those notes became known as Meditations.
First name: Marcus. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: A Roman emperor and Stoic philosopher.

hints: This female leader led a violent revolt against Roman rule in Britain.
She was queen of the Iceni people.
Her forces destroyed settlements including Londinium.
Though initially successful, her uprising was eventually crushed.
Name: Boudica.
baseClueFact: A Celtic queen who rebelled against Rome.

hints: This emperor reshaped the Roman world in the 4th century.
He won the Battle of the Milvian Bridge after a famous vision.
He favored Christianity and called the Council of Nicaea.
He founded a new imperial capital at Constantinople.
First name: Constantine. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: A Roman emperor who promoted Christianity and founded Constantinople.

hints: This feared ruler led a confederation that threatened both Roman Empires.
He gained a reputation for devastating invasions across Europe.
He was called the 'Scourge of God' by later writers.
His power collapsed soon after his death in 453.
First name: Attila. Last name starts with 'H'.
baseClueFact: The powerful leader of the Huns.

hints: This Byzantine emperor sought to restore Roman greatness.
His reign saw reconquests in North Africa and Italy.
He sponsored a major codification of Roman law.
The Hagia Sophia in Constantinople was built under his rule.
First name: Justinian. Last name starts with 'I'.
baseClueFact: A Byzantine emperor known for legal reform and building Hagia Sophia.

hints: This medieval ruler forged a large western European empire.
He was crowned emperor in Rome on Christmas Day in the year 800.
His rule promoted learning and church reform.
He became a foundational figure for both French and German history.
Name: Charlemagne.
baseClueFact: A Frankish king and emperor crowned in 800.

hints: This ruler changed English history in 1066.
He invaded from Normandy and won the Battle of Hastings.
He ordered a great survey known as the Domesday Book.
His conquest reshaped England's aristocracy and language.
First name: William. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: The Norman ruler who conquered England in 1066.

hints: This powerful medieval noblewoman was queen of both France and England.
She was one of the wealthiest and most influential women of her time.
She was mother to Richard the Lionheart and King John.
She took part in politics, patronage, and even a crusade.
First name: Eleanor. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: A medieval queen and duchess of enormous influence.

hints: This Muslim leader became famous during the Crusades.
He united major territories in Egypt and Syria.
He recaptured Jerusalem in 1187 after the Battle of Hattin.
He is often remembered for both military skill and chivalry.
Name: Saladin.
baseClueFact: A Muslim ruler who recaptured Jerusalem during the Crusades.

hints: This conqueror united the Mongol tribes into a vast war machine.
His armies created the largest contiguous land empire in history.
He relied on mobility, discipline, and ruthless strategy.
His birth name was Temujin.
First name: Genghis. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: Founder of the Mongol Empire.

hints: This grandson of a great conqueror ruled both steppe and settled empire.
He completed the Mongol conquest of China.
He founded the Yuan dynasty.
His court is described in accounts connected with Marco Polo.
First name: Kublai. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: A Mongol ruler who became emperor of China.

hints: This Venetian traveler became linked with descriptions of Asia.
He journeyed east and later dictated stories while imprisoned.
His book introduced many Europeans to the riches of the East.
He is especially associated with the court of Kublai Khan.
First name: Marco. Last name starts with 'P'.
baseClueFact: A Venetian merchant traveler associated with Asia.

hints: This Italian poet wrote one of literature's greatest journeys.
His masterpiece travels through Hell, Purgatory, and Paradise.
The first part of that work is Inferno.
He helped elevate vernacular Italian literature.
First name: Dante. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: An Italian poet best known for the Divine Comedy.

hints: This English writer helped shape Middle English literature.
He is best known for a framed collection of pilgrimage stories.
That work is The Canterbury Tales.
He wrote during the late 14th century under royal patronage.
First name: Geoffrey. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: An English poet best known for The Canterbury Tales.

hints: This medieval writer is among Europe's earliest professional women authors.
She wrote in French at the turn of the 15th century.
Her works defended women against misogynistic stereotypes.
One of her famous books is The Book of the City of Ladies.
First name: Christine. Last name starts with 'P'.
baseClueFact: A medieval author known for early feminist arguments.

hints: This inventor transformed communication in Europe.
He developed a practical movable-type printing press in Mainz.
His most famous early large work was a Latin Bible.
His innovation helped spread books, literacy, and the Reformation.
First name: Johannes. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: The German printer associated with movable type in Europe.

hints: This navigator sailed west across the Atlantic under the Spanish crown.
He reached Caribbean islands in 1492.
He believed he had reached lands near Asia.
His voyages linked Europe and the Americas in lasting and destructive ways.
First name: Christopher. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: The Genoese navigator whose 1492 voyage crossed the Atlantic.

hints: This explorer found a sea route from Europe to India around Africa.
He sailed for Portugal in the late 15th century.
His voyage rounded the Cape of Good Hope and reached Calicut.
His route transformed global trade and imperial competition.
First name: Vasco. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: The Portuguese navigator who reached India by sea.

hints: This navigator organized the first expedition to circumnavigate the globe.
He sailed under the Spanish flag despite being Portuguese.
He died in the Philippines before the voyage finished.
One ocean and one strait now bear his name.
First name: Ferdinand. Last name starts with 'M'.
baseClueFact: The explorer whose expedition first circumnavigated the Earth.

hints: This conquistador led the expedition that toppled the Aztec Empire.
He marched inland from the Gulf Coast into central Mexico.
He allied with Indigenous enemies of the Aztecs.
He captured Tenochtitlan and defeated Moctezuma's state.
First name: Hernan. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: A Spanish conquistador associated with the fall of the Aztec Empire.

hints: This conquistador overthrew a major Andean empire.
He led a small Spanish force into Peru.
He captured the Inca ruler Atahualpa.
His conquest opened the way for Spanish rule in much of South America.
First name: Francisco. Last name starts with 'P'.
baseClueFact: A Spanish conquistador associated with the fall of the Inca Empire.

hints: This Florentine thinker wrote a notorious handbook on power.
He served the Republic of Florence before Medici restoration.
His most famous short work is The Prince.
His name became linked with shrewd, ruthless political realism.
First name: Niccolo. Last name starts with 'M'.
baseClueFact: A Renaissance political thinker from Florence.

hints: This astronomer proposed that Earth moves around the sun.
He was born in the Polish city of Toruń.
His heliocentric model challenged older geocentric cosmology.
His great work was published in the year of his death, 1543.
First name: Nicolaus. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: The astronomer who advanced the heliocentric model.

hints: This theologian became central to the Protestant Reformation.
He challenged indulgences and church practices.
According to tradition, he posted Ninety-Five Theses in 1517.
He translated the Bible into German and broke with Rome.
First name: Martin. Last name starts with 'L'.
baseClueFact: A German reformer whose protests helped spark the Reformation.

hints: This reformer gave his name to a major Protestant tradition.
He worked primarily in Geneva.
His theology emphasized divine sovereignty and predestination.
His major work was the Institutes of the Christian Religion.
First name: John. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: A leading Protestant reformer based in Geneva.

hints: This English king is famous for marriage, power, and religious rupture.
He had six wives.
His break with Rome led to the English Reformation.
He dissolved monasteries and made himself supreme head of the church in England.
First name: Henry. Last name starts with 'V'.
baseClueFact: The Tudor king with six wives.

hints: This Tudor ruler presided over a celebrated English age.
She was the daughter of Henry VIII and Anne Boleyn.
England defeated the Spanish Armada during her reign.
She was known as the 'Virgin Queen.'
First name: Elizabeth. Last name starts with 'I'.
baseClueFact: The English queen associated with the Elizabethan age.

hints: This monarch's turbulent life linked Scotland, France, and England.
She was a Catholic claimant to the English throne.
She was forced to abdicate in Scotland.
Her cousin Elizabeth I eventually had her executed.
First name: Mary. Last name starts with 'S'.
baseClueFact: The Scottish queen executed after years of captivity in England.

hints: This emperor expanded and consolidated a great South Asian empire.
He ruled the Mughal Empire in the 16th century.
He became known for administrative reform and relative religious openness.
His court patronized art, learning, and architecture.
Name: Akbar.
baseClueFact: A Mughal emperor known for expansion and tolerance.

hints: This ruler presided over the Ottoman Empire at its height.
He expanded Ottoman power in Europe, the Mediterranean, and the Middle East.
He was also known as a lawgiver and patron of culture.
Westerners call him 'the Magnificent.'
First name: Suleiman. Last name starts with 'M'.
baseClueFact: An Ottoman sultan of the empire's golden age.

hints: This ruler became the first tsar of Russia.
He expanded Russian territory but ruled with extreme brutality.
His reign is associated with the oprichnina.
He is remembered for both state-building and terror.
First name: Ivan. Last name starts with 'T'.
baseClueFact: The first tsar of Russia.

hints: This Japanese leader emerged victorious after long civil wars.
He won the Battle of Sekigahara in 1600.
He founded a shogunate that ruled for over 250 years.
His government was based in Edo, later Tokyo.
First name: Tokugawa. Last name starts with 'I'.
baseClueFact: Founder of the Tokugawa shogunate.

hints: This English thinker argued for a new empirical approach to knowledge.
He served in politics as well as philosophy.
He is associated with early modern scientific method.
His essays and Novum Organum were highly influential.
First name: Francis. Last name starts with 'B'.
baseClueFact: An English philosopher linked to empiricism and scientific method.

hints: This philosopher and mathematician sought certainty through doubt.
He is famous for the statement 'I think, therefore I am.'
He also helped develop analytic geometry.
He was a major figure in rationalist philosophy.
First name: Rene. Last name starts with 'D'.
baseClueFact: A French philosopher and mathematician of the 17th century.

hints: This French thinker contributed to both mathematics and religious thought.
He worked on probability and fluid pressure.
A mechanical calculator is associated with his name.
His Pensées explored faith, reason, and the human condition.
First name: Blaise. Last name starts with 'P'.
baseClueFact: A French mathematician, inventor, and religious thinker.

hints: This astronomer discovered the mathematical laws of planetary motion.
He worked with Tycho Brahe's observational data.
He showed that planets move in ellipses rather than perfect circles.
His work helped prepare the way for Newtonian physics.
First name: Johannes. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: A German astronomer known for the laws of planetary motion.

hints: This astronomer made highly precise observations before the telescope era.
He built observatories with royal support in Denmark.
He proposed a hybrid cosmological system.
His data later helped Johannes Kepler transform astronomy.
First name: Tycho. Last name starts with 'B'.
baseClueFact: A Danish astronomer famous for precise observations.

hints: This Dutch painter became a master of light, shadow, and portraiture.
He worked during the Dutch Golden Age.
His paintings include The Night Watch and many self-portraits.
He also produced remarkable etchings and biblical scenes.
Name: Rembrandt.
baseClueFact: A Dutch Golden Age painter known for dramatic light.

hints: This Dutch painter created quiet domestic scenes of extraordinary light.
He produced relatively few works.
His paintings include Girl with a Pearl Earring and The Milkmaid.
He became world-famous long after his death.
First name: Johannes. Last name starts with 'V'.
baseClueFact: A Dutch painter known for luminous interior scenes.

hints: This Italian painter brought startling realism and intense contrast to Baroque art.
His work is known for dramatic chiaroscuro.
His life was turbulent and included violence and flight.
He painted scenes like The Calling of Saint Matthew.
Name: Caravaggio.
baseClueFact: An Italian painter famed for dramatic light and realism.

hints: This Spanish painter served at the court of Philip IV.
He painted royal portraits and complex scenes.
His most discussed masterpiece is Las Meninas.
He is considered one of the greatest painters in Western art.
First name: Diego. Last name starts with 'V'.
baseClueFact: A Spanish court painter best known for Las Meninas.

hints: This Flemish artist became a giant of Baroque painting.
His canvases are full of movement, color, and powerful bodies.
He painted mythological scenes, altarpieces, and diplomatic portraits.
His workshop influenced generations of European artists.
First name: Peter. Last name starts with 'R'.
baseClueFact: A Flemish Baroque painter and diplomat.

hints: This German composer mastered counterpoint and sacred music.
He worked as organist, cantor, and court musician.
His works include the Brandenburg Concertos and the Mass in B minor.
He is often seen as the culmination of the Baroque style.
First name: Johann. Last name starts with 'B'.
baseClueFact: A German composer central to Baroque music.

hints: This Baroque composer was born in Germany but flourished in Britain.
He composed operas, instrumental works, and oratorios.
His best-known choral work is Messiah.
One famous chorus begins with the word 'Hallelujah.'
First name: George. Last name starts with 'H'.
baseClueFact: A Baroque composer best known for Messiah.

hints: This Venetian priest and composer wrote a famous cycle of violin concertos.
That cycle depicts the seasons of the year.
He composed hundreds of concertos and many sacred works.
He was nicknamed the 'Red Priest.'
First name: Antonio. Last name starts with 'V'.
baseClueFact: An Italian Baroque composer best known for The Four Seasons.

hints: This empress presided over a major era of Russian expansion.
Born a German princess, she seized power after her husband's downfall.
She cultivated Enlightenment contacts while ruling as an autocrat.
Her reign expanded Russia into the Black Sea region.
First name: Catherine. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: The empress who ruled Russia from 1762 to 1796.

hints: This king made his state a major European power.
He ruled Prussia and won major victories in the Seven Years' War.
He admired Enlightenment culture and corresponded with Voltaire.
He is remembered as both reformer and military strategist.
First name: Frederick. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: The Prussian king who elevated his state to great-power status.

hints: This leader commanded forces in a revolution and then became a first president.
He led the Continental Army during the American Revolution.
He presided over the Constitutional Convention and served two presidential terms.
He voluntarily left office, setting an important precedent.
First name: George. Last name starts with 'W'.
baseClueFact: The first president of the United States.

hints: This American polymath was printer, inventor, writer, and statesman.
He helped draft foundational documents of a new republic.
He conducted famous electricity experiments with lightning.
He also founded civic institutions and wrote Poor Richard's Almanack.
First name: Benjamin. Last name starts with 'F'.
baseClueFact: An American inventor and statesman of the 18th century.

hints: This statesman wrote a foundational declaration in 1776.
He later became the third president of the United States.
He oversaw the Louisiana Purchase.
He founded the University of Virginia and designed his home, Monticello.
First name: Thomas. Last name starts with 'J'.
baseClueFact: An American statesman who drafted the Declaration of Independence.

hints: This revolutionary leader rose from slavery in a Caribbean colony.
He became a central figure in the Haitian Revolution.
He outmaneuvered European powers and local rivals.
Though captured before independence, he paved the way for Haiti's freedom.
First name: Toussaint. Last name starts with 'L'.
baseClueFact: A key leader of the Haitian Revolution.

hints: This revolutionary became a dominant figure in France's most radical phase.
He sat on the Committee of Public Safety.
His name is closely linked to the Reign of Terror.
He was overthrown and executed by guillotine in 1794.
First name: Maximilien. Last name starts with 'R'.
baseClueFact: A French revolutionary associated with the Reign of Terror.

hints: This Enlightenment writer and critic attacked fanaticism and injustice.
He wrote satire, history, essays, and polemics.
His novella Candide mocked easy optimism.
He became one of the best-known French philosophes.
Name: Voltaire.
baseClueFact: A French Enlightenment writer and critic.

hints: This thinker wrote powerfully about freedom, society, and education.
His work The Social Contract influenced revolutionary politics.
He also wrote Emile on education.
His line begins, 'Man is born free, and everywhere he is in chains.'
First name: Jean-Jacques. Last name starts with 'R'.
baseClueFact: A Genevan-born philosopher of the Enlightenment.

hints: This philosopher sought to explain the limits and powers of human reason.
He lived his entire life in Königsberg.
His major works include the Critique of Pure Reason.
He is central to modern ethics because of the categorical imperative.
First name: Immanuel. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: A German philosopher central to modern philosophy.

hints: This writer argued forcefully for women's education and rights.
She wrote during the era of revolutions.
Her most famous work is A Vindication of the Rights of Woman.
She was the mother of Mary Shelley.
First name: Mary. Last name starts with 'W'.
baseClueFact: An Enlightenment writer and early feminist thinker.

hints: This composer bridged the Classical and Romantic eras.
He produced symphonies, sonatas, quartets, and concertos of huge influence.
As his career progressed, he lost much of his hearing.
His Ninth Symphony ends with the 'Ode to Joy.'
Name: Beethoven.
baseClueFact: A German composer who transformed Western music.

hints: This Austrian composer worked for decades with the Esterházy family.
He helped define the symphony and string quartet.
He was a teacher of Beethoven and friend of Mozart.
He is often called the 'Father of the Symphony.'
First name: Joseph. Last name starts with 'H'.
baseClueFact: An Austrian Classical composer associated with symphonies and quartets.

hints: This Austrian composer died young but left a vast catalogue.
He wrote songs, chamber music, piano works, and symphonies.
His lieder include Erlkonig and Ave Maria.
His Symphony No. 8 is nicknamed the 'Unfinished.'
First name: Franz. Last name starts with 'S'.
baseClueFact: An Austrian composer known for lieder and lyricism.

hints: This English novelist is known for wit, courtship, and social observation.
She wrote during the late Georgian and Regency periods.
Her novels include Pride and Prejudice and Emma.
She sharply examined class, manners, and marriage.
First name: Jane. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: An English novelist known for irony and social insight.

hints: This French writer excelled in novels, poetry, and drama.
He wrote both Notre-Dame de Paris and Les Miserables.

hints: This French novelist wrote some of the most famous adventure stories ever.

hints: This writer created one of literature's defining modern myths.

hints: This journalist and political thinker became a founder of modern Zionism.

hints: This monarch gave her name to an era.

hints: This king's troubled reign ended in revolution.

First name: Franklin. Last name starts with 'R'.
baseClueFact: The U.S. president of the New Deal and World War II.

hints: This president became known for vigor, reform, and expansion of executive power.
He had earlier been a Rough Rider in the Spanish-American War.
He pursued trust-busting and conservation.
He said one should 'speak softly and carry a big stick.'
First name: Theodore. Last name starts with 'R'.
baseClueFact: An American president known for progressivism and conservation.

hints: This president represented a young image of American leadership in the early 1960s.
His presidency saw the Cuban Missile Crisis and the space race.
He delivered the line 'Ich bin ein Berliner.'
He was assassinated in Dallas in 1963.
First name: John. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: The U.S. president assassinated in 1963.

hints: This revolutionary leader founded a new state in 1949.
He led the Chinese Communist Party to victory in civil war.
His campaigns included the Great Leap Forward and Cultural Revolution.
His portrait dominates Tiananmen Gate in Beijing.
First name: Mao. Last name starts with 'Z'.
baseClueFact: The communist leader who founded the People's Republic of China.

hints: This revolutionary is often called the father of modern China.
He helped overthrow the Qing dynasty in 1911.
His political principles influenced both nationalist and later Chinese politics.
He became a symbolic founder for the Chinese republic.
First name: Sun. Last name starts with 'Y'.
baseClueFact: A revolutionary associated with the 1911 Chinese Revolution.

hints: This leader headed the Nationalist government in China.
He fought both warlords and communist rivals.
After defeat in civil war, his regime relocated to Taiwan.
He was married to Soong Mei-ling.
First name: Chiang. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: The Nationalist Chinese leader who retreated to Taiwan.

hints: This revolutionary leader became the symbolic father of modern Vietnam.
He fought French colonial rule and later became linked with the Vietnam conflict.
He proclaimed independence in 1945.
A major city was renamed in his honor after the war.
First name: Ho. Last name starts with 'M'.
baseClueFact: A Vietnamese revolutionary and founding leader.

hints: This military officer and statesman founded a republic from imperial collapse.
He led Turkish resistance after World War I.
He abolished the sultanate and launched secularizing reforms.
His surname means 'Father of the Turks.'
First name: Mustafa. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: Founder of modern Turkey.

hints: This revolutionary became a global icon of rebellion.
He fought alongside Fidel Castro in Cuba.
He later sought to export revolution abroad.
His image is one of the most reproduced portraits in the world.
First name: Che. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: A Marxist revolutionary associated with Cuba.

hints: This revolutionary ruled a Caribbean island state for decades.
He overthrew Batista in 1959.
His government aligned with the Soviet bloc during the Cold War.
The Cuban Missile Crisis occurred under his rule.
First name: Fidel. Last name starts with 'C'.
baseClueFact: The revolutionary leader of Cuba after 1959.

hints: This Indian reformer campaigned against caste oppression and for education.
He and his wife opened schools for girls and marginalized communities.
He criticized Brahmanical hierarchy and social exclusion.
He became a foundational anti-caste thinker in 19th-century India.
First name: Mahatma. Last name starts with 'P'.
baseClueFact: An Indian social reformer who fought caste oppression.

hints: This jurist and thinker fought caste inequality and shaped a nation's constitution.
He was born into a marginalized community and became a major scholar.
He chaired the drafting committee of independent India's constitution.
He later converted to Buddhism with many followers.
First name: B. Last name starts with 'A'.
baseClueFact: An Indian jurist and anti-caste leader who helped draft the constitution.

hints: This Bengali writer became a global literary figure.
He wrote poetry, songs, novels, and essays.
He won the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913.
He composed songs later adopted as national anthems in more than one country.
First name: Rabindranath. Last name starts with 'T'.
baseClueFact: A Bengali writer and Nobel laureate.

hints: This artist constantly reinvented his style across the 20th century.
He co-developed Cubism with Georges Braque.
His antiwar masterpiece Guernica responded to a bombing in Spain.
His career included Blue, Rose, Cubist, and later phases.
First name: Pablo. Last name starts with 'P'.
baseClueFact: A Spanish artist associated with Cubism and Guernica.

hints: This Dutch painter created intensely emotional works in a short career.
He painted Sunflowers and The Starry Night.
He sold little in his lifetime and struggled with mental illness.
A famous story says he mutilated part of his ear.
First name: Vincent. Last name starts with 'G'.
baseClueFact: A Dutch post-Impressionist painter known for vivid brushwork.

hints: This Mexican artist made herself the subject of powerful self-portraits.
She suffered lifelong pain after a bus accident.
Her work explored identity, body, politics, and suffering.
She was married to muralist Diego Rivera.
First name: Frida. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: A Mexican painter known for self-portraits and symbolic intensity.

hints: This artist became famous for large public murals.
He worked in Mexico and the United States.
His paintings often celebrated labor, revolution, and Indigenous heritage.
He was married to Frida Kahlo.
First name: Diego. Last name starts with 'R'.
baseClueFact: A Mexican muralist associated with public art and politics.

hints: This American artist is strongly associated with flowers, bones, and desert landscapes.
She became a central figure in American modernism.
She spent many years in New Mexico.
Her enlarged flower paintings are especially famous.
First name: Georgia. Last name starts with 'K'.
baseClueFact: An American modernist painter associated with New Mexico.
